name: jedha-cli

metadata.name: jedha-cli
metadata.summary: A CLI to start cybersecurity labs and practice your skills

documentation:
# Jedha CLI

Practice your Cybersecurity skills with Jedha CLI.

You can launch our labs directly from your terminal.

## Requirements

- Python 3.10+
- Docker
- Docker Compose
- AMD64 CPU (preferably, otherwise some labs may not work)

**This CLI is build to be used on [Kali Linux](https://www.kali.org/) priorly** and AMD64 architecture.

It may work on other Linux distributions, but we don't support them. Also it may work on Windows and MacOS but we don't support them either.

## Installation

Be sure you meet all the requirements before installing the CLI. Then use [`pipx`](https://github.com/pypa/pipx):

```bash
pipx install jedha-cli
pipx ensurepath
```

You are good to go!

## Usage

## How does it work?

The CLI will download the lab you want to launch from our servers, and then launch it using Docker Compose.

## Development

If you want to try the project from the source code, you can clone this repository and install it using:

```bash
poetry install
```

> You can install `poetry` using `pipx install poetry`.

Start the virtual environment using:

```bash
poetry shell
```

Then, you can run the CLI using:

```bash
python -m src.main --help
```

To test locally the `pip install` you can use:

```bash
poetry build
pip install dist/*.tar.gz
```

## What is Jedha?

[Jedha](https://www.jedha.co) is a Cybersecurity and Data Science bootcamp based in France. If you are interested in learning more about our bootcamps, you can visit our [website](https://www.jedha.co).

functions.name: src.misc.get_latest_version
documentation: Get the latest version of a package from PyPI.

functions.name: src.misc.current_installed_version
documentation: Get the current installed version of the package from pyproject.toml.

functions.name: src.misc.check_for_updates
documentation: Check for updates to the package.

functions.name: src.misc.get_lab_config_file
documentation:
Get the content of the lab's Docker Compose configuration file.
Args:
    labname (str): The name of the lab.
Returns:
    str: The content of the lab's Docker Compose configuration file.

functions.name: src.misc.run_command
documentation:
Utility function to run a command and return True if it succeeds.

Args:
    command (List[str]): The command to run as a list of strings.
Returns:
    bool: True if the command succeeds, False otherwise.

functions.name: src.misc.docker_is_installed
documentation:
Check if Docker is installed on the machine.

Returns:
    bool: True if Docker is installed, False otherwise.

functions.name: src.misc.docker_compose_v2_is_installed
documentation:
Check if Docker Compose V2 is installed on the machine.

Returns:
    bool: True if Docker Compose is installed, False otherwise.

functions.name: src.misc.docker_compose_v1_is_installed
documentation:
Check if Docker Compose V1 is installed on the machine.

Returns:
    bool: True if Docker Compose is installed, False otherwise.

functions.name: src.misc.docker_requires_sudo
documentation:
Check if Docker requires sudo.

Returns:
    bool: True if Docker requires sudo, False otherwise.

functions.name: src.misc.get_docker_compose_command
documentation:
Manage the Docker command depending on the OS.

Args:
    args (List[str]): The arguments to pass to the Docker compose command.

functions.name: src.misc.is_docker_running
documentation:
Check if Docker is running.

Returns:
    bool: True if Docker is running, False otherwise.

functions.name: src.misc.get_running_labs
documentation:
Get the list of running labs.

Returns:
    set[str]: The list of running labs.

functions.name: src.misc.is_lab_already_running
documentation:
Check if one lab is already running.

Args:
    verbose (bool, optional): Whether to print a message if a lab is already running. Defaults to True.
Returns:
    bool: True if one lab at least is already running, False otherwise.

functions.name: src.misc.clean_docker_network
documentation: Clean Docker Network using `prune` to avoid conflicting IP range issues.

functions.name: src.misc.cleanup_lab
documentation:
Cleanup the lab by removing the containers and networks.

Args:
    labname (str): The name of the lab.
    lab_config_file (str): The path to the lab's Docker Compose configuration.

modules.name: src.main
documentation: ! /usr/bin/python3

functions.name: src.main.list
documentation: List all the labs available.
@app.command("config", help="Configure the CLI.")
def config():
Configure the CLI by prompting the user for the required information.
pass

functions.name: src.main.download
documentation:
Download a lab.

Args:
    labnames (List[str]): List of the lab names.

functions.name: src.main.status
documentation:
Show the list of running labs.

If a lab name is provided, it will show the status of that lab.

Args:
    labname (Optional[str]): Name of the lab.

functions.name: src.main.start
documentation:
Start a lab.

Args:
    labname (str): Name of the lab.

functions.name: src.main.restart
documentation:
Restart a lab.

Args:
    labname (str): Name of the lab.

functions.name: src.main.stop

params.name: typer.Option(prompt="Are you sure you want to stop the lab?")

documentation:
Stop and clean up a specific lab environment.

Args:
    labname (str): Name of the lab.

functions.name: src.main.remove

params.name: typer.Option(prompt="Are you sure you want to remove the lab?")

documentation:
Remove a lab.

Args:
